heading: Budget treatment for energy conservation measures
label: 8255 Budget treatment for energy conservation measures
notes: References in TextThe Energy Policy and Conservation Act, referred to in par. (2), is , , , which is classified principally to chapter 77 (§ 6201 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. Amendments1992— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Each agency, in support of the President’s annual budget request to the Congress, shall specifically set forth and identify funds requested for energy conservation measures.”Pub. L. 102–4861988— amended section generally, substituting provision relating to budget treatment for energy conservation measures for provisions relating to establishment and use of life cycle cost methods, use of life cycle costs, and use in non-Federal structures.Pub. L. 100–6151986—Subsec. (a)(2). substituted “average” for “marginal”.Pub. L. 99–5091980—Subsec. (a)(1). , which directed amendment of par. (1) by inserting provisions setting forth criteria for establishing life-cycle costs for Federal buildings before the period at end, was executed to par. (2) as the probable intent of Congress because par. (1) does not contain a period.Pub. L. 96–294

children.text: electric and other energy costs to be incurred in operating and maintaining agency facilities; and

compliance with the provisions of this part, the Energy Policy and Conservation Act ( et seq.), and all applicable Executive orders, including Executive Order 12003 ( note) and Executive Order 12759 (56 Fed. Reg. 16257).42 U.S.C. 620142 U.S.C. 6201
